segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: Yes.
segments.zh: 是的。这颗牙齿是一个叫拉塞尔·格雷厄姆的人发现的。他从一个洞穴的岩石下捡起来。他知道这很特别——首先它保存得非常好，就像刚从动物颌骨上拔下来一样。总之，他们发现它有6500年历史了。

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: The tooth was found by a man called Russell Graham.

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: He picked it up from under a rock in a cave.

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: He knew it was special – for a start it was in really good condition, as if it had been just extracted from the animal's jawbone.

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: Anyway, they found it was 6,500 years old.

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: So why was that significant?
segments.zh: 那为什么这很重要呢？

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: Well the mammoth bones previously found on the North American mainland were much less recent than that.
segments.zh: 嗯，之前在北美洲大陆发现的猛犸象骨头都没有这么晚近。所以这真的很惊人。

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: So this was really amazing.

segments.speaker: MARTIN
segments.en: Then we're making an animated diagram to show the geography of the area in prehistoric times.
segments.zh: 然后我们正在制作一个动画图表，展示史前时期该地区的地理状况。所以最初圣保罗岛并不是一个岛，它与大陆相连，猛犸象和熊等其他动物可以在整个区域自由活动。

segments.speaker: MARTIN
segments.en: So originally, St Paul's Island wasn't an island, it was connected to the mainland, and mammoths and other animals like bears were able to roam around the whole area.

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: Then the climate warmed up and the sea level began to rise, and the island got cut off from the mainland.
segments.zh: 然后气候变暖，海平面开始上升，岛屿与大陆分离。所以岛上的那些猛犸象无法逃脱：它们不得不留在岛上。

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: So those mammoths on the island couldn't escape: they had to stay on the island.

segments.speaker: MARTIN
segments.en: And in fact the species survived there for thousands of years after they'd become extinct on the mainland.
segments.zh: 事实上，这个物种在大陆上灭绝后，还在岛上存活了数千年。

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: So why do you think they died out on the mainland?
segments.zh: 那你认为它们为什么在大陆上灭绝了呢？

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: No one's sure.
segments.zh: 没人确定。

segments.speaker: MARTIN
segments.en: Anyway, next we'll explain how Graham and his team identified the date when the mammoths became extinct on the island.
segments.zh: 接下来，我们会解释格雷厄姆和他的团队是如何确定岛上猛犸象灭绝的日期的。他们得出结论，灭绝发生在5600年前，这对于史前灭绝来说是一个非常精确的时间。这是基于他们从岛上湖底采集的泥样。他们分析了这些样本，以了解随着时间的推移，哪些东西落入了湖中——植物碎片、火山灰，甚至猛犸象自身的DNA。这是标准程序，但花了将近两年的时间。

segments.speaker: MARTIN
segments.en: They concluded that the extinction happened 5,600 years ago, which is a very precise time for a prehistoric extinction.

segments.speaker: MARTIN
segments.en: It's based on samples they took from mud at the bottom of a lake on the island.

segments.speaker: MARTIN
segments.en: They analysed it to find out what had fallen in over time – bits of plants, volcanic ash and even DNA from the mammoths themselves.

segments.speaker: MARTIN
segments.en: It's standard procedure, but it took nearly two years to do.

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: So why don't you quickly go through the main sections of your presentation and discuss what action's needed for each part?
segments.zh: 那你为什么不快速过一遍你演示的主要部分，讨论一下每个部分需要做什么呢？

segments.speaker: MARTIN
segments.en: OK.
segments.zh: 好的。对于引言部分，我们打算用视觉辅助，所以一旦准备好，这部分就完成了。

segments.speaker: MARTIN
segments.en: So for the introduction, we're using a visual, so once we've prepared that we're done.

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: I'm not sure.
segments.zh: 我不确定。我觉得我们需要把想在这里包含的所有想法都写下来，不能只靠记忆。演示的开头部分非常重要……

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: I think we need to write down all the ideas we want to include here, not just rely on memory.

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: How we begin the presentation is so important ...

segments.speaker: MARTIN
segments.en: You're right.
segments.zh: 你说得对。

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: The discovery of the mammoth tooth is probably the most dramatic part, but we don't have that much information, only what we got from the online article.
segments.zh: 猛犸象牙齿的发现可能是最精彩的部分，但我们没有那么多信息，只有从在线文章中获得的内容。我想也许我们可以联系领导该团队的研究员，请他多告诉我们一些。

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: I thought maybe we could get in touch with the researcher who led the team and ask him to tell us a bit more.

segments.speaker: MARTIN
segments.en: Great idea.
segments.zh: 好主意。那关于研究人员最初提出的问题那部分呢？我们有很多内容，但需要让它变得有趣。

segments.speaker: MARTIN
segments.en: What about the section with the initial questions asked by the researchers?

segments.speaker: MARTIN
segments.en: We've got a lot on that but we need to make it interesting.

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: We could ask the audience to suggest some questions about it and then see how many of them we can answer.
segments.zh: 我们可以请观众提出一些问题，然后看看我们能回答多少。我觉得不会花太长时间。

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: I don't think it would take too long.

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: Yes that would add a bit of variety.
segments.zh: 是的，那会增添一些变化。

segments.speaker: MARTIN
segments.en: Then the section on further research carried out on the island – analysing the mud in the lake.
segments.zh: 然后是岛上进一步研究的部分——分析湖里的泥。我在想我们这里的信息是不是太多了，要不要删掉一些？

segments.speaker: MARTIN
segments.en: I wonder if we've actually got too much information here, should we cut some?

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: I don't think so, but it's all a bit muddled at present.
segments.zh: 我不这么认为，但目前有点混乱。

segments.speaker: MARTIN
segments.en: Yes, maybe it would be better if it followed a chronological pattern.
segments.zh: 是的，也许按时间顺序排列会更好。

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: I think so.
segments.zh: 我也这么认为。发现和可能解释的部分基本准备好了，但我们需要练习一下，确保不会超时。

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: The findings and possible explanations section is just about ready, but we need to practise it so we're sure it won't overrun.

segments.speaker: MARTIN
segments.en: I think it should be OK, but yes, let's make sure.
segments.zh: 我觉得应该没问题，但好的，我们确认一下。

items.prompt: How will Rosie and Martin introduce their presentation?
What was surprising about the mammoth tooth found by Russell Graham?

pairs.note: surprising 和 amazing 是同义替换，都表示令人惊讶。
tooth 和 bones 都是猛犸象的遗骸，属于同义替换。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到 'mammoth bones' 和 'amazing'，与题干中的 'mammoth tooth' 和 'surprising' 对应，通过同义替换定位答案。

items.prompt: The students will use an animated diagram to demonstrate how the mammoths

pairs.p: couldn't escape: they had to stay
pairs.note: 题干说'展示猛犸象如何...'，原文用'无法逃脱，只能待在岛上'解释了结果，属于同义转述。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问'展示猛犸象如何...'，定位句说明'它们无法逃脱，只能待在岛上'，对应选项A（被困在岛上）。

items.prompt: According to Martin, what is unusual about the date of the mammoths' extinction on the island?

pairs.note: unusual 在这里指不同寻常，原文用 precise 强调精确，两者都表示这个日期很特别。
原文直接给出了灭绝时间，与题干中的 extinction 和 island 对应。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问的是灭绝日期有什么不寻常之处，定位句提到这个日期非常精确（a very precise time），这就是 unusual 的同义替换。

pairs.note: 题干Introduction被替换为原文中'How we begin the presentation'，表示演讲的开头部分。

paraphrase.explain: 题干Introduction对应原文中的'How we begin the presentation'，通过同义替换定位到答案E。

pairs.p: The discovery of the mammoth tooth
pairs.note: 题干与原文完全一致，直接定位。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中的'Discovery of the mammoth tooth'在原文中逐字出现，直接定位到该句。

items.prompt: Initial questions asked by the researchers

pairs.note: 题干中的'Initial questions'对应原文中的'questions'，但原文没有'initial'，所以是部分同义。
题干说'由研究者提问'，原文说'我们可以让观众提问'，动作主体不同但意思相关。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'ask the audience to suggest some questions'，与题干'Initial questions asked by the researchers'不完全一致，但通过'questions'和'ask'可以定位到该句。

items.prompt: Further research carried out on the island

pairs.q: Further research carried out on the island
pairs.note: 题干说‘在岛上进行进一步研究’，原文说‘按时间顺序排列’，两者是不同说法，但都指向同一内容。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中的‘Further research carried out on the island’对应原文中的‘followed a chronological pattern’，通过同义替换定位到答案H。

pairs.p: The findings and possible explanations section
pairs.note: 题干与原文几乎完全一致，直接定位到该部分。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中的'Findings and possible explanations'在原文中几乎原样出现，因此可以直接定位到该部分。
